manifest.name: desktop-commander
manifest.description: Build, explore, and automate on your local machine with access to files and terminal.
manifest.long_description:
Combine local filesystem access with full terminal control to handle technical tasks through natural language. Desktop Commander empowers you to build, explore, and automate - from organizing repositories to creating complete applications:
* **Build from scratch** - Create features and applications with simple commands
* **Manage development environments** - Set up servers, configure systems, and handle processes
* **Manage context and documentation** - Keep track of project details and technical specifications
* **Explore existing codebases and projects** - Navigate and understand complex repositories

This extension bridges technical skill gaps by providing full command-line superpowers through an interface that understands your intent and handles complexity automatically.

tools.name: get_config
tools.description: Get the complete server configuration including blocked commands, allowed directories, file limits, telemetry settings, client info, and system information.

tools.name: set_config_value
tools.description: Set a specific configuration value by key. WARNING: Should be used in a separate chat from file operations to prevent security issues.

tools.name: read_file
tools.description: Read contents from local filesystem, URLs, Excel files (.xlsx, .xls, .xlsm), PDFs, and DOCX files with line/page-based pagination.

tools.name: read_multiple_files
tools.description: Read the contents of multiple files simultaneously. Handles text files and renders images.

tools.name: write_file
tools.description: Write or append to file contents. Supports Excel files (JSON 2D array format) and DOCX creation from markdown. For PDFs, use write_pdf instead.

tools.name: write_pdf
tools.description: Create new PDF files from markdown or modify existing PDFs (insert/delete pages). Supports HTML/CSS styling and SVG graphics.

tools.name: create_directory
tools.description: Create a new directory or ensure a directory exists. Can create multiple nested directories in one operation.

tools.name: list_directory
tools.description: Get a detailed recursive listing of all files and directories in a specified path. Supports depth parameter (default: 2) for multi-level directory traversal.

tools.name: move_file
tools.description: Move or rename files and directories between locations.

tools.name: start_search
tools.description: Start streaming search for files by name or content patterns. Searches text files, Excel, and DOCX content.

tools.name: get_more_search_results
tools.description: Get more results from an active search with offset-based pagination.

tools.name: stop_search
tools.description: Stop an active search gracefully when you've found what you need.

tools.name: list_searches
tools.description: List all active searches with their status and runtime information.

tools.name: get_file_info
tools.description: Retrieve detailed metadata about a file or directory including size, timestamps, permissions, line counts, and sheet info for Excel files.

tools.name: edit_block
tools.description: Apply targeted text replacements for text and DOCX files, or range-based cell updates for Excel files.

tools.name: start_process
tools.description: Start a new terminal process with intelligent state detection. Primary tool for local file analysis (CSV, JSON, logs, etc.).

tools.name: read_process_output
tools.description: Read output from a running process with intelligent completion detection, REPL prompt recognition, and offset/length pagination.

tools.name: interact_with_process
tools.description: Send input to a running process and automatically receive the response. Critical tool for all local file analysis.

tools.name: force_terminate
tools.description: Force terminate a running terminal session.

tools.name: list_sessions
tools.description: List all active terminal sessions with status information including blocked state and runtime.

tools.name: list_processes
tools.description: List all running processes with PID, command name, CPU usage, and memory usage.

tools.name: kill_process
tools.description: Terminate a running process by PID. Use with caution.

tools.name: get_usage_stats
tools.description: Get usage statistics for debugging and analysis including tool usage and performance metrics.

tools.name: get_recent_tool_calls
tools.description: Get recent tool call history with their arguments and outputs. Returns chronological list of tool calls made during this session for debugging, context recovery, and onboarding new chats.

tools.name: give_feedback_to_desktop_commander
tools.description: Open feedback form in browser to provide feedback about Desktop Commander.

tools.name: get_prompts
tools.description: Browse and retrieve curated Desktop Commander prompts for various tasks and workflows.

manifest.name: pdf-toolkit
manifest.display_name: PDF Tools - Fill, Analyze, Extract, View
manifest.description: Let Claude work with PDFs on your computer: fill forms, analyze documents, extract data, and view them interactively — no uploading required.
manifest.long_description:
The complete PDF toolkit for Claude Desktop. Work with PDFs already on your computer without uploading.

**Fill Forms & Automate:**
• Fill out W-9, 1099, I-9, rental applications, and any fillable PDF
• Bulk fill hundreds of PDFs from CSV data
• Save reusable profiles for common forms
• Validate required fields before submission

**Interactive PDF Viewer:**
• View any PDF with page navigation, zoom, and fullscreen
• Search text across all pages with highlighted results
• Select and copy text directly from PDFs
• Form field sidebar shows all fields with fill status

**Analyze & Extract:**
• Analyze research papers and academic documents
• Extract tables and structured data to CSV
• Compare contract versions
• Summarize 300+ page reports

**Works with any PDF type:** Scientific papers, legal contracts, technical manuals, financial statements, invoices, and forms. Handles fillable forms, scanned documents, and encrypted PDFs.

**Perfect for:** Researchers analyzing literature, lawyers comparing contracts, engineers reviewing specifications, accountants processing tax forms, and businesses automating invoices.

tools.name: display_pdf
tools.description: Display an interactive PDF viewer with search, navigation, zoom, and form field detection

tools.name: list_pdfs
tools.description: List all PDF files in a directory

tools.name: read_pdf_fields
tools.description: Read all form fields from a PDF file

tools.name: fill_pdf
tools.description: Fill a PDF form with provided data and save it

tools.name: bulk_fill_from_csv
tools.description: Fill multiple PDFs using data from a CSV file

tools.name: save_profile
tools.description: Save form data as a reusable profile

tools.name: load_profile
tools.description: Load a saved profile

tools.name: list_profiles
tools.description: List all saved profiles

tools.name: fill_with_profile
tools.description: Fill a PDF using a saved profile

tools.name: extract_to_csv
tools.description: Extract form data from filled PDFs to a CSV file

tools.name: validate_pdf
tools.description: Validate if all required fields in a PDF are filled

tools.name: read_pdf_content
tools.description: Read and analyze the full content of a PDF file - extract text, summarize, convert to other formats, or answer questions about the document

tools.name: get_pdf_resource_uri
tools.description: Get a resource URI for a PDF file that can be used with Claude's built-in PDF reading capabilities via the Resources API

prompts.name: view_and_analyze_pdf
prompts.description: Open and analyze any PDF document
prompts.text: I'll open your PDF in the interactive viewer and analyze it, focusing on ${arguments.focus}. I can extract key findings, summarize sections, answer questions about specific pages, pull out structured data, and identify form fields if present.

prompts.name: research_paper_analysis
prompts.text: I'll open your research paper in the interactive viewer and analyze it, focusing on ${arguments.focus_area}. I can extract key findings and methodology, identify research gaps, create chapter-by-chapter summaries, pull out all citations, and generate a literature review outline.

prompts.name: contract_comparison
prompts.text: I'll compare multiple contracts or legal documents, focusing on ${arguments.comparison_focus}. I'll highlight changed clauses, modified terms, added/removed sections, pricing differences, liability changes, and create a comparison summary.

prompts.name: financial_report_extraction
prompts.text: I'll extract and analyze data from your financial PDFs including all tables and key metrics. I'll convert the data to ${arguments.output_format} format for analysis, with specific page references for all extracted information.

prompts.name: document_qa_session
prompts.text: Let's discuss your PDF document. Starting with: ${arguments.initial_question}. I can find specific information, explain complex sections, cross-reference different parts, extract quotes with page numbers, and answer 'what if' scenarios.

prompts.name: bulk_invoice_processing
prompts.description: Process multiple invoices into structured data
prompts.text: I'll process all invoice PDFs in ${arguments.folder_path} and create a unified dataset in ${arguments.output_format} format. I'll extract vendor names, invoice numbers, dates, line items, totals, tax amounts, and payment terms.

prompts.name: technical_documentation_summary
prompts.text: I'll analyze your technical documentation and create ${arguments.summary_type}. I'll extract code examples, create reference guides, identify implementation steps, and summarize troubleshooting sections.

prompts.name: fill_w9_business
prompts.text: I'll open your W-9 in the interactive viewer so you can see it, then fill it for your business. I need: Business legal name, DBA/trade name (if different), business type (LLC/Corp/Partnership), tax classification, EIN, and business address. I'll properly check the correct tax classification boxes and ensure the form meets IRS requirements.

prompts.name: batch_invoices
prompts.text: I'll help you batch process invoices. Point me to the folder containing your invoice PDFs. I can: extract all amounts and dates, create a summary spreadsheet, identify missing information, calculate totals by vendor/date/category. What specific data do you need extracted?

prompts.name: rental_application
prompts.text: I'll open your rental application in the viewer and help complete it. Have ready: personal info, employment history (3 years), income verification, previous addresses (3 years), references, vehicle info, and emergency contact. I'll ensure all required fields are filled and flag any that need supporting documents.

prompts.name: extract_1099_data
prompts.text: I'll extract all data from your 1099 forms (1099-NEC, 1099-MISC, 1099-DIV, 1099-INT, etc.) and create a tax summary. I'll organize by payer, identify the type of income, sum totals by category, and format it for easy import into tax software.

manifest.name: Filesystem
manifest.description: Let Claude access your filesystem to read and write files.
manifest.long_description:
This extension allows Claude to interact with your local filesystem, enabling it to read and write files directly. This can be useful for tasks such as file management, data processing, and automation of repetitive tasks. The extension provides a set of tools that can be used to navigate directories, read file contents, and write new files or modify existing ones.

Underneath the hood, it uses @modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem.

tools.name: read_file
tools.description: Read the contents of a file

tools.name: read_multiple_files
tools.description: Read the contents of multiple files

tools.name: write_file
tools.description: Write content to a file

tools.name: edit_file
tools.description: Edit the contents of a file

tools.name: create_directory
tools.description: Create a new directory

tools.name: list_directory
tools.description: List contents of a directory

tools.name: directory_tree
tools.description: Display directory structure as a tree

tools.name: move_file
tools.description: Move or rename a file

tools.name: search_files
tools.description: Search for files by name or content

tools.name: get_file_info
tools.description: Get information about a file

tools.name: list_allowed_directories
tools.description: List directories that can be accessed

allowed_directories.title: Allowed Directories
allowed_directories.description: Select directories the filesystem server can access

manifest.name: Windows-MCP
manifest.description: MCP Server that enables Claude to interact with Windows OS
manifest.long_description:
Windows-MCP is an open-source project that enables seamless integration between AI agents and the Windows operating system. Acting as an MCP server, it bridges the gap between large language models (LLMs) and the Windows OS, allowing agents to perform tasks such as **file navigation, application control, UI interaction, QA testing, and more**.

**KEY FEATURES**
- **Seamless Windows Integration**: Interacts natively with Windows UI elements, opens applications, controls windows, simulates user input, and more.
- **Use Any LLM (Vision Optional)**: Does not rely on traditional computer vision techniques or fine-tuned models. Works with any LLM, reducing complexity and setup time.
- **Rich Toolset for UI Automation**: Includes tools for keyboard and mouse control, window management, and capturing window or UI state.
- **Lightweight & Open-Source**: Minimal dependencies with full source code available under the MIT license.
- **Customizable & Extendable**: Easily adapt or extend tools to suit custom automation workflows or AI integrations.
- **Real-Time Interaction**: Typical latency between actions ranges from `0.2` to `0.9` seconds, depending on system load, active applications, and LLM inference speed.

**MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS**
- **Python 3.13+**
- **UV Package Manager** — `curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh`

tools.name: App
tools.description: Manages Windows applications with three modes: 'launch' (opens the prescibed application), 'resize' (adjusts active window size/position), 'switch' (brings specific window into focus).

tools.name: Shell
tools.description: A comprehensive system tool for executing any PowerShell commands. Use it to navigate the file system, manage files and processes, and execute system-level operations. Capable of accessing web content, interacting with network resources, and performing complex administrative tasks.

tools.name: File
tools.description: Manages file system operations with eight modes: 'read' (read text file contents), 'write' (create/overwrite/append to files), 'copy' (copy files or directories), 'move' (move or rename), 'delete' (delete files or directories), 'list' (list directory contents), 'search' (find files by glob pattern), 'info' (get metadata like size, dates, type). Relative paths are resolved from the user's Desktop folder. Use absolute paths to access other locations.

tools.name: Snapshot
tools.description: Captures complete desktop state including: system language, focused/opened windows, interactive elements (buttons, text fields, links, menus with coordinates), and scrollable areas. Set use_vision=True to include screenshot. Set use_dom=True for browser content to get web page elements instead of browser UI. Always call this first to understand the current desktop state before taking actions.

tools.name: Click
tools.description: Performs mouse clicks at specified coordinates [x, y] or passing a UI element's label/id. Supports button types: 'left' for selection/activation, 'right' for context menus, 'middle'. Supports clicks: 0=hover only (no click), 1=single click (select/focus), 2=double click (open/activate). Provide either loc or label.

tools.name: Type
tools.description: Types text at specified coordinates [x, y] or passing a UI element's label/id. Set clear=True to clear existing text first, False to append. Set press_enter=True to submit after typing. Set caret_position to 'start' (beginning), 'end' (end), or 'idle' (default). Provide either loc or label.

tools.name: Scroll
tools.description: Scrolls at coordinates [x, y], a UI element's label/id, or current mouse position if loc=None. Type: vertical (default) or horizontal. Direction: up/down for vertical, left/right for horizontal. wheel_times controls amount (1 wheel ≈ 3-5 lines). Use for navigating long content, lists, and web pages.

tools.name: Move
tools.description: Moves mouse cursor to coordinates [x, y] or passing a UI element's label/id. Set drag=True to perform a drag-and-drop operation from the current mouse position to the target coordinates. Default (drag=False) is a simple cursor move (hover). Provide either loc or label.

tools.name: Shortcut
tools.description: Executes keyboard shortcuts using key combinations separated by +. Examples: "ctrl+c" (copy), "ctrl+v" (paste), "alt+tab" (switch apps), "win+r" (Run dialog), "win" (Start menu), "ctrl+shift+esc" (Task Manager). Use for quick actions and system commands.

tools.name: Wait
tools.description: Pauses execution for specified duration in seconds. Use when waiting for: applications to launch/load, UI animations to complete, page content to render, dialogs to appear, or between rapid actions. Helps ensure UI is ready before next interaction.

tools.name: Scrape
tools.description: Fetch content from a URL or the active browser tab. By default, performs a lightweight HTTP request to the URL. If you need to extract text from the active tab's DOM within the viewport, ensure the page is open in a browser and use Snapshot with use_dom=True first, then the agent will handle extraction.

tools.name: MultiSelect
tools.description: Selects multiple items such as files, folders, or checkboxes if press_ctrl=True, or performs multiple clicks if False. Pass locs (list of coordinates) or labels (list of UI element labels/ids).

tools.name: MultiEdit
tools.description: Enters text into multiple input fields at specified coordinates locs=[[x,y,text], ...] or using labels=[[label,text], ...]. Provide either locs or labels.

tools.name: Clipboard
tools.description: Manages Windows clipboard operations. Use mode="get" to read current clipboard content, mode="set" to set clipboard text.

tools.name: Process
tools.description: Manages system processes. Use mode="list" to list running processes with filtering and sorting options. Use mode="kill" to terminate processes by PID or name.

tools.name: Notification
tools.description: Sends a Windows toast notification with a title and message. Useful for alerting the user remotely.

tools.name: Registry
tools.description: Accesses the Windows Registry. Use mode="get" to read a value, mode="set" to create/update a value, mode="delete" to remove a value or key, mode="list" to list values and sub-keys under a path.

mode.title: Mode (local or remote)
mode.description: Set to 'local' to run directly on your machine, or 'remote' to use cloud-hosted Windows automation via windowsmcp.io. Spin up a Windows sandbox in seconds with no local setup needed. Remote mode requires a Sandbox ID and API Key from the dashboard at windowsmcp.io.

sandbox_id.title: Sandbox ID
sandbox_id.description: The sandbox/VM identifier from the windowsmcp.io. Required when mode is set to 'remote'.

api_key.title: API Key
api_key.description: Your API key from the windowsmcp.io. Required when mode is set to 'remote'.

anonymized_telemetry.title: Anonymized Telemetry
anonymized_telemetry.description: Windows-MCP collects basic usage data to help improve the MCP server. No personal information, tool arguments, or tool outputs are tracked.
